title: Formal Methods: Foundations and Applications
subtitle: An Integrated Understanding of Specification, Model Checking, and Theorem Proving
description: A practical introduction to mathematically rigorous software design, from specification and model checking to theorem proving and program verification.
author: Kazuhiko Ota (ITDO Inc.)

chapters.title: Chapter 1: Why Formal Methods Matter
chapters.description: Why modern software needs mathematically grounded reasoning, with lessons from failures and safety expectations.

chapters.title: Chapter 2: Bridging Mathematics and Programs
chapters.description: How mathematical thinking connects to programming, with models of state and change.

chapters.title: Chapter 3: Foundations of Specification
chapters.description: From ambiguity in natural language to precise specifications with preconditions, postconditions, and invariants.

chapters.title: Chapter 4: Introduction to Lightweight Formal Methods — Specification with Alloy
chapters.description: Alloy's modeling style and practical verification with relational reasoning.

chapters.title: Chapter 5: State-Based Specification — Fundamentals of Z Notation
chapters.description: States, schemas, and precise specification in Z notation.

chapters.title: Chapter 6: Process-Centric Specification — Concurrency with CSP
chapters.description: Concurrency, communication, and deadlock analysis with CSP.

chapters.title: Chapter 7: Specifying Time — Introduction to TLA+
chapters.description: Dynamic system properties with temporal logic and TLA+.

chapters.title: Chapter 8: Introduction to Model Checking
chapters.description: Automated verification, state-space exploration, and counterexamples.

chapters.title: Chapter 9: Fundamentals of Theorem Proving
chapters.description: Mechanized proof, type theory, and proof assistants such as Coq.

chapters.title: Chapter 10: Program Verification
chapters.description: Program correctness with Hoare logic, loop invariants, and verified reasoning about arrays.

chapters.title: Chapter 11: Integrating Formal Methods into Development Processes
chapters.description: Incremental adoption strategies and organizational integration.

chapters.title: Chapter 12: Tools and Automation
chapters.description: Tool ecosystems, CI integration, and operational automation.

chapters.title: Chapter 13: Case Studies
chapters.description: Detailed studies of industrial adoption, including rail, security, and large-scale distributed systems.

appendices.title: Appendix A: Mathematics Refresher
appendices.description: A concise review of sets, logic, and functions.

appendices.title: Appendix B: Tool Setup and Verification Quick Start
appendices.description: The shortest reproducible path to running the companion examples for Alloy, TLC, Apalache, Dafny, and Lean 4.

appendices.title: Appendix C: Notation Cross-Reference
appendices.description: A comparison of symbols and syntax across Z, Alloy, TLA+, and related notations.

appendices.title: Appendix D: Exercise Guides and Self-Review Frameworks
appendices.description: Reusable answer structures and self-review criteria for the end-of-chapter exercises.

appendices.title: Appendix E: References and Further Reading Paths
appendices.description: Primary sources and chapter-aligned paths for deeper follow-up reading.

appendices.title: Appendix F: Practical Playbook for AI-Assisted Formal Methods
appendices.description: A workflow playbook for using AI assistance while preserving verification evidence and reviewability.
